groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Name, format, category, audience, goal, facts, benefits, brand, destination, tone, length.

groups.title: Product Details & Proof
groups.description: Features, specs, materials, package, usage, evidence, and claim limits.

groups.title: Offer & Purchase Information
groups.description: Variants, price, delivery, returns, digital access, subscription terms.

groups.title: Positioning, SEO & Brand
groups.description: Customer problem, differentiators, use cases, objections, SEO, voice, extras.

variables.label: Product name
variables.placeholder: Example: AeroPress Go Travel Coffee Maker
variables.hint: Enter the exact product name or working name that should appear in the copy.

help.why: The product cannot be identified or titled accurately without its name.
help.example: AeroPress Go Travel Coffee Maker
help.avoid: Vague working titles that customers will never see.

variables.label: Product format
variables.placeholder: Select the product format
variables.hint: This controls which product-detail fields and purchase information are relevant.

help.why: Physical, digital, subscription, and bundled products require different purchase information.
help.example: Physical product
help.avoid: Picking a format that does not match fulfilment reality.

variables.label: Product category
variables.placeholder: Example: Portable coffee maker, skincare serum, online course
variables.hint: Describe the product category clearly enough to establish context and relevant claim rules.

help.why: The category determines terminology, customer expectations, structure, and potential regulated-claim concerns.
help.example: Example: Portable coffee maker, skincare serum, online course
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Target customer
variables.placeholder: Who is most likely to buy this product, and what matters to them?
variables.hint: Describe the intended customer, situation, experience level, or buying priorities.

help.why: Product value must be translated into benefits that matter to a specific customer.
help.example: Who is most likely to buy this product, and what matters to them?
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main goal
variables.hint: Choose the main job the description should perform.

help.why: The goal affects emphasis, structure, detail level, CTA strength, and SEO treatment.
help.example: Drive direct sales
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Essential product facts
variables.placeholder: Explain what it is, how it works, its main components, and verified capabilities.
variables.hint: Include only facts that may be stated publicly and accurately.

help.why: The description requires a reliable factual foundation and must not infer unsupported capabilities.
help.example: What it is, how it works, verified capabilities only
help.avoid: Unreleased features stated as available.

variables.label: Main customer benefits
variables.placeholder: What useful outcomes or improvements does the product provide?
variables.hint: Focus on supported customer value rather than exaggerated promises.

help.why: Benefits explain why the supplied facts and features matter to the buyer.
help.example: Faster morning coffee on the road without a bulky setup
help.avoid: Guaranteed outcomes without evidence.

variables.label: Brand name
variables.placeholder: Example: Northfield Supply
variables.hint: Leave blank when the brand should not be mentioned.

help.why: The brand may need to appear in titles, descriptions, or CTA wording.
help.example: Example: Northfield Supply
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Store or destination
variables.placeholder: Example: Shopify store, WooCommerce page, retail catalogue
variables.hint: Name the intended store, page type, or sales environment when known.

help.why: The destination affects formatting, length, title style, SEO elements, and purchase-detail placement.
help.example: Online store product page
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Select a tone
variables.hint: Choose the overall writing style without changing the underlying facts.

help.why: Tone materially changes phrasing and positioning while a safe default reduces user effort.
help.example: Clear and natural
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Select the output language
variables.hint: The complete generated package will use this language.

help.why: Language cannot always be inferred reliably from product data or the publishing destination.
help.example: English
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Description length
variables.placeholder: Select a preferred length
variables.hint: Auto chooses an appropriate depth for the product, destination, and sales goal.

help.why: Different stores and purchase decisions require materially different description depths.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Key features
variables.placeholder: List the most important functions, components, or design features.
variables.hint: Include features that should appear separately from the main narrative.

help.why: A feature list supports scanning and prevents important capabilities from being buried.
help.example: List the most important functions, components, or design features.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Technical specifications
variables.placeholder: Dimensions, weight, capacity, compatibility, power, model, or other specifications.
variables.hint: Enter exact specifications only; unsupported values will not be estimated.

help.why: Physical products often require precise specifications before a customer can purchase confidently.
help.example: Dimensions, weight, capacity, compatibility, power, model, or other specifications.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Materials or ingredients
variables.placeholder: List relevant materials, ingredients, composition, sourcing, or allergen details.
variables.hint: Include exact terminology and any qualification that must remain attached to a claim.

help.why: Composition can affect product suitability, trust, safety, care, and purchase decisions.
help.example: List relevant materials, ingredients, composition, sourcing, or allergen details.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: What is included
variables.placeholder: List package contents, files, modules, accessories, licences, or bonuses.
variables.hint: Clarify exactly what the customer receives and what is not included when important.

help.why: Clear package contents reduce misunderstanding and avoid implying that optional items are included.
help.example: List package contents, files, modules, accessories, licences, or bonuses.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Use, setup or care
variables.placeholder: Explain setup, use, care, compatibility, prerequisites, or safe handling.
variables.hint: Add only information that a buyer should know before or shortly after purchase.

help.why: Some products require setup, maintenance, compatibility, or safe-use context to be described accurately.
help.example: Explain setup, use, care, compatibility, prerequisites, or safe handling.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Approved proof and credentials
variables.placeholder: Verified tests, certifications, awards, ratings, research, reviews, or measurable results.
variables.hint: Include only approved evidence and enough context to state it accurately.

help.why: Evidence can strengthen credibility but must be supplied rather than invented.
help.example: Verified tests, certifications, awards, ratings, research, reviews, or measurable results.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Claims and restrictions
variables.placeholder: Claims to avoid, required disclaimers, legal wording, limitations, or sensitive topics.
variables.hint: Use this for regulated products, restricted claims, and important product limitations.

help.why: The product owner knows which statements are prohibited, qualified, jurisdiction-specific, or commercially sensitive.
help.example: No medical claims; no “clinically proven” without study
help.avoid: Leaving regulated claim bans only in your head.

variables.label: Variants and options
variables.placeholder: Sizes, colours, flavours, plans, editions, quantities, or customisation choices.
variables.hint: Describe only variants that are currently available or intended for publication.

help.why: Variants may change purchase information, suitability wording, and product-title treatment.
help.example: Sizes, colours, flavours, plans, editions, quantities, or customisation choices.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Price or offer details
variables.placeholder: Price, valid discount, bundle saving, tax note, or offer conditions.
variables.hint: Leave blank to omit price and promotional claims.

help.why: Pricing and promotions must be supplied exactly and must never be fabricated.
help.example: Price, valid discount, bundle saving, tax note, or offer conditions.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Availability and delivery
variables.placeholder: Stock status, processing time, delivery method, region, access timing, or fulfilment limits.
variables.hint: Include only current or approved fulfilment information.

help.why: Delivery and availability can materially affect a buyer’s decision and must remain accurate.
help.example: Stock status, processing time, delivery method, region, access timing, or fulfilment limits.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Warranty and returns
variables.placeholder: Warranty length, return period, exclusions, trial conditions, or support policy.
variables.hint: State exact terms or leave blank; the model will not invent a policy.

help.why: Purchase protections and limitations should be described only from supplied policy details.
help.example: Warranty length, return period, exclusions, trial conditions, or support policy.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Digital access details
variables.placeholder: File formats, download method, licence, device access, updates, or access duration.
variables.hint: Explain how the customer receives and may use the digital product.

help.why: Digital products require access, format, licence, and compatibility information instead of physical shipping details.
help.example: File formats, download method, licence, device access, updates, or access duration.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Subscription terms
variables.placeholder: Billing frequency, renewal, cancellation, minimum term, trial, and included access.
variables.hint: Include exact recurring-payment terms and avoid implied conditions.

help.why: Recurring products require transparent billing, renewal, cancellation, and access information.
help.example: Billed monthly; cancel anytime; what renews
help.avoid: Invented renewal or cancellation policy.

variables.label: Customer need or problem
variables.placeholder: What situation, frustration, task, or need leads the customer to seek this product?
variables.hint: Use a realistic customer need without exaggerating fear or discomfort.

help.why: The buying context helps connect the product to a relevant and credible customer need.
help.example: What situation, frustration, task, or need leads the customer to seek this product?
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Key differentiators
variables.placeholder: What makes this product meaningfully different from common alternatives?
variables.hint: Use verifiable differences and avoid unsupported superiority claims.

help.why: Distinctive positioning cannot be inferred safely from generic category knowledge.
help.example: What makes this product meaningfully different from common alternatives?
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Use cases
variables.placeholder: Describe realistic situations, environments, tasks, or customer scenarios.
variables.hint: Include only uses the product is designed or approved for.

help.why: Concrete scenarios help customers assess fit without inventing unsupported applications.
help.example: Describe realistic situations, environments, tasks, or customer scenarios.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Purchase concerns
variables.placeholder: Common questions about fit, difficulty, price, compatibility, quality, or delivery.
variables.hint: Add concerns the description should resolve without becoming defensive.

help.why: Known objections can be answered naturally using verified product information.
help.example: Common questions about fit, difficulty, price, compatibility, quality, or delivery.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Search keywords
variables.placeholder: Primary phrase and a few closely related terms, separated by commas.
variables.hint: Leave blank for natural category language without deliberate keyword targeting.

help.why: User-supplied search terms help align the copy with an existing SEO strategy without keyword guessing.
help.example: Primary phrase and a few closely related terms, separated by commas.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Brand voice notes
variables.placeholder: Describe preferred vocabulary, personality, formality, and phrases to use or avoid.
variables.hint: Add guidance beyond the selected tone only when the brand has a distinct voice.

help.why: Specific brand conventions cannot be inferred from a general tone selection.
help.example: Describe preferred vocabulary, personality, formality, and phrases to use or avoid.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add any important instruction not covered elsewhere.
variables.hint: Use for essential context only; standard writing and proofreading rules are already applied.

help.why: One bounded catch-all field supports legitimate specialised requirements without creating several miscellaneous inputs.
help.example: Add any important instruction not covered elsewhere.
help.avoid: Invented specs, prices, reviews, scarcity, or performance guarantees.
